name: DigitalOcean API
what_it_does: DigitalOcean's REST API for managing Droplets (VMs), Kubernetes clusters, managed databases, App Platform deployments, spaces (object storage), and networking.
use_cases: Scaling Kubernetes clusters and node pools via agents
Managing App Platform deployments and environment variables
Managing DNS records, load balancers, and firewalls via automation
not_for: Very large-scale enterprise deployments needing premium SLAs
Specialized AI/ML workloads requiring GPU (limited availability)
Teams needing on-premise or hybrid cloud deployments
best_when: An agent needs to manage DigitalOcean infrastructure including VMs, Kubernetes, and managed databases with a clean, developer-friendly API.
avoid_when: You need deep AWS/GCP/Azure service integrations, or require specialized enterprise cloud features.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens (full account access or read-only). OAuth2 for third-party integrations. Token-based auth is very simple for agents. Read-only tokens useful for monitoring agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $200 in free credits for new accounts (60-day expiry)
pricing.paid_tiers: Droplets from $4/month, Kubernetes from $12/month/node, Managed DB from $15/month
pricing.notes: Very transparent and competitive pricing. No hidden fees. Pricing is simpler than AWS/GCP. Credit card required. $200 credit covers extensive testing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with id and message fields. Error IDs are unique codes that map to documentation. Very agent-friendly error format. OpenAPI spec is well-maintained.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Droplet creation is not idempotent by default. Some resources use names as unique keys. Kubernetes node pool operations use IDs for idempotent updates.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 250/5 min can be hit by agents provisioning many resources quickly
Droplet creation is asynchronous — agents must poll action status for completion
No native idempotency keys — agents should check for existing resources before creating
Floating IP and reserved IP concepts coexist — different API resources
SSH key must be pre-added to account before specifying in Droplet creation
Snapshot operations can take many minutes — agents need long polling timeouts
